rationale: Keep: the notes indicate strong conceptual overlap in core constructs (intertemporal choice, present bias, time-horizon sensitivity), with the cited 2006 paper providing a key framing/concreteness account that is directly relevant to the citing 2011 paper’s claims about reduced present bias and waiting behavior. The citing studies also use defer/expedite-style timing trade-offs (e.g., expediting premiums across short vs. long delays), making this plausibly a substantive prior mechanism/comparison reference rather than a generic mention; the DOI match confirms it is explicitly cited.
